title: Throw errors when using bogus --network flags
body:
Currently if you give a bogus --network flag,
buildah just ignores it and continues on.

This change will throw proper errors.

Add tests to make sure it works correctly.

Also remove some stuttering to clean up error messages.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

There was an issue about podman build --net=container ... not working, which I can not find.  I did not think that was legal.  And while testing it, I tried --net=container1 and it did not blow up, thus this PR.

--net=container is actually supposed to work, although it is no longer documented.  In previous versions of buildah, it was the equivalent is --net=private.
code changes LGTM. Looks like a rebase is needed

LGTM
can you please rebase though, I think this is probably not out of date, but I'd like to be sure.
